proof: function[T0: FiniteGroup] { map[T0, Set[T0]](T0.elements, finite_conjugacy_class_set[T0]).unique = finite_conjugacy_class_sets[T0] }[G]

goal: exists(k0: G) { G.elements.contains(k0) and finite_conjugacy_class_set(k0) = c }
exists(k0: G) { k0 = x and finite_conjugacy_class_set(k0) = c }

proof: let w0: G satisfy { exists(k0: G) { k0 = w0 and finite_conjugacy_class_set(k0) = c } }
let w1: G satisfy { w0 = w1 and finite_conjugacy_class_set(w1) = c }
let w2: G satisfy { w0 = w2 and finite_conjugacy_class_set(w2) = c }
function(x0: G) { finite_conjugacy_class_set(x0) != c }(w2)
function(x0: Set[K], x1: Set[K]) { not x0 ∈ items or not x1 ∈ items or x0.is_disjoint(x1) or x0 = x1 }(c, d)

goal: items.contains(c) and items.contains(d) and c != d implies c.is_disjoint(d)
set_list_pairwise_disjoint[K](items) = forall(x0: Set[K], x1: Set[K]) { items.contains(x0) and items.contains(x1) and x0 != x1 implies x0.is_disjoint(x1) }
proof: function[T0](x0: List[Set[T0]]) { forall(x1: Set[T0], x2: Set[T0]) { not x1 ∈ x0 or not x2 ∈ x0 or x2 = x1 or x1.is_disjoint(x2) } = set_list_pairwise_disjoint[T0](x0) }[K](items)

let w1: Set[K] satisfy { w0 ∈ items and w1 ∈ items and w0 != w1 and not w0.is_disjoint(w1) }

goal: finite_conjugacy_class_sets[G].contains(c) and finite_conjugacy_class_sets[G].contains(d) and c != d implies c.is_disjoint(d)

proof: let w0: Set[G] satisfy { exists(k0: Set[G]) { w0 ∈ finite_conjugacy_class_sets[G] and k0 ∈ finite_conjugacy_class_sets[G] and k0 != w0 and not w0.is_disjoint(k0) } }
let w1: Set[G] satisfy { w0 ∈ finite_conjugacy_class_sets[G] and w1 ∈ finite_conjugacy_class_sets[G] and w0 != w1 and not w0.is_disjoint(w1) }
let w2: Set[G] satisfy { w0 ∈ finite_conjugacy_class_sets[G] and w2 ∈ finite_conjugacy_class_sets[G] and w0 != w2 and not w0.is_disjoint(w2) }
function[T0: FiniteGroup](x0: Set[T0], x1: Set[T0]) { not x0 ∈ finite_conjugacy_class_sets[T0] or not x1 ∈ finite_conjugacy_class_sets[T0] or x0.is_disjoint(x1) or x0 = x1 }[G](w0, w2)

goal: classes.is_unique and set_list_pairwise_disjoint[G](classes) and set_list_all_cardinality_by[G](classes, card)
list_indexed_union[G, Set[G]](classes, function(x0: Set[G]) { x0 }).cardinality_is(set_list_cardinality_sum[G](classes, card))

proof: function[T0: FiniteGroup](x0: Set[T0] -> Nat) { set_list_cardinality_sum[T0](finite_conjugacy_class_sets[T0], x0) = finite_conjugacy_class_cardinality_sum[T0](x0) }[G](card)
